summary: Excellent soundboard capture of the tight‑early‑Dead 1966 show, highlighted by crisp “Stealin’” and “Lindy”.
review: These reviews rave about a pristine soundboard source that brings out extra treble, minimal hiss and sharp, clean licks. The performance itself is praised as the band’s tightest early work, with “Stealin'” and “Lindy” singled out as standout moments. Overall the recording and show are considered essential listening for 1966 Dead fans.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is ~4.3, most comments emphasize superb audio fidelity and strong early‑Dead performance; only one neutral 3‑star review, giving a solid high‑positive overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, extra treble, very little hiss or clicking; licks are sharp and clean. Reviewers note this version sounds superior to other releases.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Early‑1966 set captured at its tightest; the band sounds focused and rehearsed, making for a cohesive, energetic performance.
